subject: Human Resources
title: Internal vs. External Hiring
description: A fast breakdown of Internal vs. External Hiring for builders.

hook.text: A company fills every senior role with outsiders. Internal employees stop trying—there's nowhere to grow.

buildup.text: Internal hiring promotes from within. External hiring brings fresh perspectives from outside.

discovery.text: Internal hires ramp up 30% faster and are less likely to leave. External hires bring new skills and ideas.

twist.text: Too much internal hiring creates echo chambers. Too much external hiring destroys loyalty. Balance is key.

climax.text: The best companies do both strategically—growing talent while selectively adding outside expertise.

punchline.text: Grow from within. Import what you're missing.

quiz.question: What is an advantage of internal hiring?
options: Internal hires ramp up faster and are more likely to stay
It eliminates the need for recruitment
